dataset: U.S. Department of State Travel Advisories

pi_items.source: U.S. Embassy Cairo official address: 5 Tawfik Diab Street, Garden City, Cairo, Egypt (https://eg.usembassy.gov/contact/)
U.S. Embassy Tokyo official address: 1-10-5 Akasaka, Minato-ku, Tokyo 107-8420 Japan (https://jp.usembassy.gov/contact/)
U.S. Embassy New Delhi official address: Shantipath, Chanakyapuri New Delhi - 110021 India (https://in.usembassy.gov/new-delhi/)

U.S. Department of State Egypt travel page: Visa required for U.S. citizens. Options include e-visa or visa on arrival (https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/us-visas/Visa-Reciprocity-and-Civil-Documents-by-Country/Egypt.html)

U.S. Department of State Japan travel page: U.S. citizens do not need a visa for short-term visits (up to 90 days) for tourism, business, or visiting friends/family (https://jp.usembassy.gov/services/visas-japan-u-s-citizens/)

U.S. Department of State India travel page: Indian visa is required for most foreign nationals including U.S. citizens. Options include e-Tourist visa and regular tourist visa (https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/us-visas/Visa-Reciprocity-and-Civil-Documents-by-Country/India.html)
Task description specifies these three countries for research
Official U.S. Department of State travel advisory website (https://travel.state.gov/)

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims for this task have been verified against authoritative U.S. Department of State sources. The task requires research on travel.state.gov for three countries: Egypt (visa required, embassy at Tawfik Street in Cairo), Japan (visa not required for short-term stays, embassy at Akasaka in Tokyo), and India (visa required, embassy at Shantipath in New Delhi). All address keywords and visa requirement expectations match the official embassy locations and visa policies provided by the State Department.
